ud1.10:1.2: at one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta's Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika's park.
ud1.10:1.3: Now at that time Bāhiya Dārucīriya was residing at Suppāraka on the seashore, honored, respected, esteemed, venerated, revered, and receiving robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicinal requisites for the sick.
ud1.10:1.4: Then, while Bāhiya Dārucīriya was alone in retreat, this thought arose in his mind [*cetaso*]:
ud1.10:1.5: "Whoever in the world are *arahants* or have entered upon the path to arahantship, I am one of them."
ud1.10:10.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, having instructed Bāhiya Dārucīriya with this brief instruction, departed.
ud1.10:10.2: Then, not long after the *Bhagavā* had departed, a cow with a young calf attacked Bāhiya Dārucīriya and deprived him of life.
ud1.10:11.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, having walked for alms in Sāvatthī, after the meal, returning from his alms round, left the city together with several *bhikkhus* and saw Bāhiya Dārucīriya dead;
ud1.10:11.2: having seen him, he addressed the *bhikkhus*:
ud1.10:11.3: "Take Bāhiya Dārucīriya's body, *bhikkhus*; place it on a bier, carry it out, cremate it, and make a stūpa for him.
ud1.10:11.4: Your fellow in the holy life, *bhikkhus*, has died."
ud1.10:12.1: "Yes, *bhante*," those *bhikkhus* replied to the *Bhagavā*. Having placed Bāhiya Dārucīriya's body on a bier, carried it out, cremated it, and made a stūpa for him, they went to the *Bhagavā*; having gone, they paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat to one side.
ud1.10:12.2: Sitting to one side, those *bhikkhus* said this to the *Bhagavā*:
ud1.10:12.3: "Bāhiya Dārucīriya's body has been burned, *bhante*, and a stūpa has been made for him.
ud1.10:12.4: What is his destination, what is his future course?"
ud1.10:12.5: "Bāhiya Dārucīriya was wise, *bhikkhus*. He practiced in accordance with the *dhamma* in line with the *dhamma*, and he did not trouble me on account of the *dhamma*.
ud1.10:13.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, understanding this matter, at that time uttered this inspired utterance:
ud1.10:14.2: fire and air gain no footing;
ud1.10:14.3: There the stars do not shine,
ud1.10:14.5: There the moon does not glow,
ud1.10:15.1: And when the sage has known for himself,
ud1.10:16.1: This inspired utterance too was spoken by the *Bhagavā*; thus have I heard.
ud1.10:17.4: and with Bāhiya, these are the ten.
ud1.10:2.1: Then a *devatā* who had formerly been a blood-relative of Bāhiya Dārucīriya, compassionate and wishing for his welfare, knowing with her mind [*cetasā*] the thought in Bāhiya Dārucīriya's mind [*cetoparivitakkaṁ*], went to Bāhiya Dārucīriya; having gone, she said this to Bāhiya Dārucīriya:
ud1.10:2.2: "You are not an *arahant*, Bāhiya, nor have you entered upon the path to arahantship.
ud1.10:2.3: Nor do you have that practice by which you might be an *arahant* or have entered upon the path to arahantship."
ud1.10:3.1: "Then who now, in the world with its devas, are *arahants* or have entered upon the path to arahantship?"
ud1.10:3.2: "There is, Bāhiya, in the northern countries, a city named Sāvatthī.
ud1.10:3.3: There the *Bhagavā* now dwells, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one.
ud1.10:3.4: For that *Bhagavā*, Bāhiya, is an *arahant* and teaches the *dhamma* for arahantship."
ud1.10:4.1: Then Bāhiya Dārucīriya, stirred by that *devatā*, set out from Suppāraka right away.
ud1.10:4.2: Staying only one night everywhere, he went to Sāvatthī, to Jeta's Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika's park.
ud1.10:4.3: Now at that time several *bhikkhus* were walking up and down in the open air.
ud1.10:4.4: Then Bāhiya Dārucīriya went to those *bhikkhus*; having gone, he said this to those *bhikkhus*:
ud1.10:4.5: "Where, *bhante*, is the *Bhagavā* now dwelling, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one?
ud1.10:4.6: We wish to see that *Bhagavā*, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one."
ud1.10:4.7: "The *Bhagavā*, Bāhiya, has entered among the houses for alms."
ud1.10:5.1: Then Bāhiya Dārucīriya, in haste, left Jeta's Grove, entered Sāvatthī, and saw the *Bhagavā* walking for alms in Sāvatthī: graceful, inspiring confidence, with peaceful *indriyas*, peaceful in mind [*santamānasaṁ*], having attained the highest taming and settling [*samatha*], tamed, guarded, with restrained *indriyas*, a nāga.
ud1.10:5.2: Having seen him, he went to the *Bhagavā*; having gone, he fell with his head at the *Bhagavā*'s feet and said this to the *Bhagavā*:
ud1.10:5.3: "Let the *Bhagavā* teach me the *dhamma*, *bhante*; let the Fortunate One teach the *dhamma*,
ud1.10:5.4: so that it may be for my welfare and *sukha* for a long time."
ud1.10:5.5: When this was said, the *Bhagavā* said this to Bāhiya Dārucīriya:
ud1.10:5.6: "It is not the right time yet, Bāhiya; we have entered among the houses for alms."
ud1.10:6.1: For a second time Bāhiya Dārucīriya said this to the *Bhagavā*:
ud1.10:6.2: "This cannot be known, *bhante*: the dangers to the *Bhagavā*'s life or the dangers to my life.
ud1.10:6.4: let the Fortunate One teach the *dhamma*, so that it may be for my welfare and *sukha* for a long time."
ud1.10:6.5: For a second time the *Bhagavā* said this to Bāhiya Dārucīriya:
ud1.10:6.6: "It is not the right time yet, Bāhiya; we have entered among the houses for alms."
ud1.10:7.1: For a third time Bāhiya Dārucīriya said this to the *Bhagavā*:
ud1.10:7.2: "This cannot be known, *bhante*: the dangers to the *Bhagavā*'s life or the dangers to my life.
ud1.10:7.4: let the Fortunate One teach the *dhamma*, so that it may be for my welfare and *sukha* for a long time."
ud1.10:8.1: "Therefore, Bāhiya, you should train yourself thus:
ud1.10:8.10: This itself is the end of *dukkha*."
ud1.10:8.2: ‘In the seen there will be only the seen; in the heard there will be only the heard; in the sensed there will be only the sensed; in the cognized there will be only the cognized.’
ud1.10:8.3: Thus, Bāhiya, you should train yourself.
ud1.10:8.4: When, Bāhiya, in the seen there will be only the seen; in the heard there will be only the heard; in the sensed there will be only the sensed; in the cognized there will be only the cognized,
ud1.10:8.5: then, Bāhiya, you will not be by that.
ud1.10:8.6: When, Bāhiya, you are not by that,
ud1.10:8.7: then, Bāhiya, you will not be there.
ud1.10:8.8: When, Bāhiya, you are not there,
ud1.10:8.9: then, Bāhiya, you will be neither here nor beyond nor between the two.
ud1.10:9.1: Then, through this brief teaching of the *dhamma* by the *Bhagavā*, Bāhiya Dārucīriya's mind [*cittaṁ*] was immediately liberated from the *āsavas* through non-*upādāna*.
